correct_answer: Ibuprofen is contraindicated in the third trimester due to risk of premature closure of the fetal ductus arteriosus and oligohydramnios. Paracetamol is preferred.
Metronidazole is generally considered safe in pregnancy, including first trimester, when clinically indicated.
Isotretinoin is absolutely contraindicated due to severe teratogenicity. Must stop at least 1 month before conception.
Warfarin is contraindicated in pregnancy due to fetal warfarin syndrome. Heparin is preferred.
ACE inhibitors are contraindicated in pregnancy due to fetal renal failure and oligohydramnios.
Warfarin and clarithromycin is a major interaction causing increased INR and bleeding risk.
Sertraline and tramadol increases risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures. Avoid combination.
Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4 increasing simvastatin levels causing risk of rhabdomyolysis.
Metformin should be withheld in patients receiving contrast if renal function risk exists due to lactic acidosis.
Linezolid with SSRIs increases risk of serotonin syndrome due to MAOI activity.
Metformin is contraindicated in CKD stage 4 if eGFR <30 due to lactic acidosis risk.
Paracetamol can be used cautiously in liver disease at reduced doses.
Gentamicin requires dose adjustment and monitoring in renal impairment due to toxicity risks.
Enoxaparin requires dose adjustment in CKD due to accumulation and bleeding risk.
Rifampicin should be used cautiously due to hepatotoxicity and requires monitoring.
Amoxicillin dose is 80–90 mg/kg/day divided doses.
Ibuprofen dose in children is 5–10 mg/kg every 6–8 hours.
Paracetamol dose is 10–15 mg/kg every 4–6 hours.
Aspirin should be avoided in children due to risk of Reye's syndrome.
Chloramphenicol should be avoided in neonates due to gray baby syndrome.
Propranolol is contraindicated in asthma due to bronchospasm risk.
Primaquine is contraindicated in G6PD deficiency due to hemolysis risk.
Diphenhydramine should be avoided in elderly due to anticholinergic effects and falls.
Sildenafil with nitrates causes severe hypotension and is contraindicated.
Long-term metoclopramide use can cause tardive dyskinesia and should be limited.
Benzodiazepines should be avoided in elderly due to falls and cognitive impairment.
Insulin overdose causes hypoglycemia leading to confusion, seizures, and coma.
Opioids in elderly should be started at low doses due to respiratory depression risk.
Anticholinergics worsen cognitive impairment and should be avoided in dementia.
NSAIDs should be avoided in CKD due to worsening renal function.
